# Research Summary: okr-goal-setting-stinger

**Date:** 2026-05-20
**Pipeline phase:** 1.5 (between command-center and stinger-forge)

---

- **Depth tier:** normal
- **Time window:** 2025-11-20 to 2026-05-20 (6 months), plus foundational canon sources (Grove/Doerr) regardless of date
- **Page budget consumed:** ~11 primary sources, ~150 pages of content processed

## 5 most influential sources

### 1. Devokr Complete OKR Guide 2026 (`external/01-devokr-complete-okr-guide-2026.md`)
*Why it matters:* The most comprehensive up-to-date practitioner reference (OKR-BOK certified author, 2026-labeled). Covers the full Grove→Doerr lineage, output-vs-input discipline, 0.6-0.7 scoring philosophy, and a phased implementation roadmap. Has citation-ready statistics (Deloitte 31% return premium, HBR 3.5x engagement). This is the single best overview document for stinger-forge to anchor guides/01 through guides/04.

### 2. Devokr OKR vs KPI vs MBO [2026] (`external/02-devokr-okr-vs-kpi-vs-mbo-2026.md`)
*Why it matters:* Contains a verbatim 6-row comparison table covering Purpose / Cycle / Success Threshold / Direction / Mid-cycle Changes / Ambition Level. The car analogy (KPI = fuel gauge, OKR = navigation, MBO = annual roadmap) is memorable and citation-ready. The MBO compensation-linkage contrast is the most precise source for the Command Brief's "never link OKRs to compensation" directive. This source directly builds guides/01-okr-canon.md's comparison matrix.

### 3. Radical Focus — Wodtke summary (`external/03-radical-focus-wodtke-small-team.md`)
*Why it matters:* Wodtke is the third canonical author in the OKR intellectual lineage (Grove, Doerr, Wodtke) and the primary source for small-team/startup adaptation patterns. Unique contributions: the weekly confidence rating system (not scoring, but team's subjective probability of hitting the KR by end of quarter), the health metric guardrail pattern (what you must not break while chasing the Objective), and the Exploratory OKR for pre-PMF startups. Entire guides/06-small-team-adaptation.md is grounded in this source.

### 4. OKR Scoring: Stretch vs Commit (OKRS.com, Feb 2026) (`external/04-okrs-scoring-calibration-2026.md`)
*Why it matters:* The most precise treatment of the aspirational-vs-committed OKR distinction and the three scoring models (Radical Focus binary / Measure What Matters 0.7 / Stretch-Target-Commit three-threshold). The Command Brief's critical directive — "always distinguish aspirational OKRs from committed OKRs BEFORE applying the 70% rule" — is backed by this source. The sandbagging root causes (compensation linkage, fear of failure, cultural risk-aversion) complete the calibration guide.

### 5. OKR Pitfalls: 7 Common Mistakes (OKRS.com, March 2026) (`external/10-okr-pitfalls-anti-patterns-2026.md`)
*Why it matters:* The most actionable practitioner source for the "are these actually OKRs?" audit. Contains 4 citation-ready input→output KR rewrite examples, the 7 pitfall checklist that maps directly to an audit report scorecard, and the single most cited organizational failure mode (OKR theater). This source is the raw material for both guides/03-writing-key-results.md and examples/weak-to-strong-rewrite.md.

---

## 5 open questions (unresolved by research)

1. **Google re:Work OKR guide is gone (404).** The canonical public-facing Google OKR resource referenced in the Command Brief no longer exists at its URL. Stinger-forge should check `https://rework.withgoogle.com` for a new URL or use `https://www.whatmatters.com` (Doerr's official OKR site) as the replacement canonical online reference.

2. **Cascade guide: include or exclude?** The Command Brief explicitly asks this question. Research found no strong practitioner consensus on whether to include cascade guidance for small-team adaptations. The evidence leans toward "exclude by default, note as advanced practice": Weekdone's bottom-up model and Wodtke's single-OKR pattern both suggest cascade adds overhead without proportional benefit below ~50 people. Stinger-forge should make the call or ask the user.

3. **The 70% moonshot rule: default or advanced option?** The Command Brief flags this as unresolved. Research shows it is the Google/Doerr default for aspirational OKRs, but the broader practitioner community uses it inconsistently. The three-model framework from source 04 (Radical Focus binary / Doerr 0.7 / Stretch-Target-Commit) gives stinger-forge a structure to present all three without prescribing one as the only answer.

4. **Lattice and 15Five current OKR configuration docs.** Both reference URLs from the Command Brief returned 404. Stinger-forge must directly visit the current Lattice and 15Five help centers to capture the current OKR field mapping and cycle setup workflow before authoring guides/07-tools.md.

5. **Exploratory OKRs for pre-PMF startups.** Wodtke introduces this concept (qualitative, discovery-mode OKRs before you have metrics) but the research found limited practitioner depth on it. Stinger-forge may need to reference Wodtke's website (cwodtke.com) directly or the second edition of Radical Focus for more detail on this pattern.

---

## Sources stinger-forge should re-fetch with deeper context

1. **`https://www.whatmatters.com`** — John Doerr's official OKR resource site. This is the living digital companion to "Measure What Matters" and likely contains the CFR templates, OKR examples, and grading rubrics that the old Google re:Work guide no longer hosts.
2. **`https://www.15five.com/okr-software/`** (or equivalent current URL) — 15Five OKR configuration docs for guides/07-tools.md
3. **`https://help.lattice.com`** (OKRs/Goals section) — Lattice OKR configuration docs for guides/07-tools.md
4. **`https://cwodtke.com`** — Christina Wodtke's website for the Exploratory OKR pattern and any 2025-2026 blog posts updating Radical Focus patterns

---

## Domain synthesis note for stinger-forge

The OKR canon has three tiers:

**Tier 1 — Primary canon (non-negotiable citations):**
- Andy Grove, "High Output Management" (the iMBO origin)
- John Doerr, "Measure What Matters" (the modern formalization, CFR, Google case study)
- Laszlo Bock, "Work Rules!" (the 70% moonshot rule, compensation decoupling at Google)
- Christina Wodtke, "Radical Focus" (startup adaptation, confidence rating, health metrics)

**Tier 2 — Practitioner synthesis (current, citable):**
- Devokr guides (OKR-BOK certified, 2026) — best current overview
- OKRS.com pitfalls + scoring guides (2026) — most actionable anti-pattern coverage
- Weekdone learning center — best tool-integrated methodology guide

**Tier 3 — Tool docs (verify before citing, UX changes frequently):**
- Lattice help center (Goals/OKRs section)
- 15Five OKR docs
- Weekdone OKR wizard documentation

The stinger's normative claims must be anchored in Tier 1. Tier 2 sources provide current practitioner framing and examples. Tier 3 sources provide configuration procedures only — never normative claims.
